commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P2] The remaining blockers are narrow schema/docs/test and audit repairs that an automated fix PR can attempt; final host-read approval remains a maintainer decision after that repair.; Needs attention: The diff intentionally adds an authenticated Gateway path to host filesystem reads, so security-owner approval is still needed even after the mechanical blockers are fixed.; Review finding: [P1] Add the directInvo... (sha=e598e2d762837b96efb44334397e3f904de8cfb1)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a dual-key `gateway.tools.directInvoke.hostFsRead` opt-in to expose the `read` coding tool through the shared Gateway HTTP/RPC direct-invoke resolver.

PR surface: Source +166, Tests +73, Docs +50. Total +289 across 7 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review blockers: source inspection shows the PR documents and types `gateway.tools.directInvoke.hostFsRead`, while the PR-head zod schema still rejects unknown keys under strict `gateway.tools`. The audit false positive is also source-reproducible from the added `read` default-deny entry and the existing generic `dangerous_allow` filter.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The remaining blockers are narrow schema/docs/test and audit repairs that an automated fix PR can attempt; final host-read approval remains a maintainer decision after that repair.

target.title: feat(gateway): wire read coding tool into HTTP /tools/invoke (narrow)
target.body:
Closes #66561 (orthogonal â€” direct-invoke read benefits the embedded run-budget cascade); narrow first landing of the broader direct-invoke umbrella tracked in #37131.

## Summary

Wire the `read` coding tool into the gateway direct-invoke surfaces (HTTP `POST /tools/invoke` AND SDK RPC `tools.invoke`, which share the resolver) so it is reachable for deterministic automation â€” CI/preflight checks, lint pipelines, browser capture flows â€” without an LLM round-trip.

**This PR is the redesigned narrow read-only landing.** The prior 10-commit history was reset to a single fresh commit on top of current upstream `7c1a83ff2e`, restructured to satisfy ClawSweeper's [P1] from the prior review: **dual-key gating** so the existing `gateway.tools.allow: ["read"]` config knob does not silently grant host-FS read on upgrade.

## Dual-key gating (addresses ClawSweeper [P1])

To enable `read` on the direct-invoke surface, operators must set BOTH:

| Key | Purpose | Default |
|---|---|---|
| `gateway.tools.directInvoke.hostFsRead: true` | **NEW.** Distinct surface-specific opt-in. Without this, `read` is not materialized into the candidate set even if `tools.allow` includes it. | `false` (omitted) |
| `gateway.tools.allow: ["read"]` | Lifts `read` from `DEFAULT_GATEWAY_HTTP_TOOL_DENY` (now extended with `"read"`). Existing knob; keeps operator-acceptance step visible in the standard policy surface. | not present |

**Either key alone leaves `read` unreachable.** Old configs that already contain `allow: ["read"]` for unrelated reasons (e.g. an MCP/agent surface where `read` was always available) stay inert at the direct-invoke layer â€” no silent upgrade-time expansion.

| `tools.allow` includes `"read"` | `directInvoke.hostFsRead` | `read` reachable on direct-invoke |
|---|---|---|
| no  | no   | âťŚ |
| yes | no   | âťŚ (tool not materialized) |
| no  | yes  | âťŚ (filtered by HTTP deny) |
| yes | yes  | âś… |

## Scope

- **Read-only.** Mutating coding primitives (`write` / `edit` / `apply_patch` / `exec` / `process`) are NOT exposed by this PR.
- **Both surfaces.** Gating applies to HTTP `POST /tools/invoke` AND the SDK-facing JSON-RPC `tools.invoke` (they share `resolveGatewayScopedTools`).
- **MCP loopback unaffected.** `surface === "loopback"` is not gated.
- **Hook-bypass safe.** Uses new `createOpenClawCodingToolsRaw` export (module-private Symbol marker, not reachable from typed SDK params) so HTTP-surface tools aren't double-wrapped â€” `handleToolsInvokeHttp` already runs `runBeforeToolCallHook` itself before dispatch.

## Files changed

- `src/agents/agent-tools.ts` â€” `SKIP_BEFORE_TOOL_CALL_HOOK` symbol + `createOpenClawCodingToolsRaw` export
- `src/config/types.gateway.ts` â€” `GatewayToolsConfig.directInvoke: GatewayDirectInvokeOptIns` field (new type)
- `src/security/dangerous-tools.ts` â€” `"read"` added to `DEFAULT_GATEWAY_HTTP_TOOL_DENY`
- `src/gateway/tool-resolution.ts` â€” dual-key gated materialization of `read` into candidate set
- `src/security/audit-gateway-config.ts` â€” `gateway.tools_invoke_http.host_read_allow` finding (only fires when BOTH gates set; silent for either alone since `read` is unreachable there)
- `src/security/audit-gateway-tools-http.test.ts` â€” 5 new test cases covering all 4 gate combinations + vanilla
- `docs/gateway/tools-invoke-http-api.md` â€” operator-facing dual-key opt-in docs with config example + truth table

## Real-behavior proof on head `e598e2d762`

**Build:** `node:22-bookworm-slim` arm64-native container, `node scripts/tsdown-build.mjs && node scripts/runtime-postbuild.mjs`. Output bundle `dist/tool-resolution-tMi-Eqaq.js` confirmed to contain `directInvoke` + `hostFsRead` strings (also in `dist/audit-DTRmpXNZ.js`).

**Driver:** Node ESM script imports `resolveGatewayScopedTools` from the freshly-compiled bundle (resolved by `fn.name`), calls it with 4 config shapes, asserts whether `read` is in the returned tools list.

```
[bundle] /work/dist/tool-resolution-tMi-Eqaq.js
[resolve] resolveGatewayScopedTools found

[CASE 1: neither gate set (vanilla)]                                     read=false  tools=23  âś…
[CASE 2: only allow:['read'] set (legacy shape)]                         read=false  tools=23  âś…
[CASE 3: only directInvoke.hostFsRead=true set]                          read=false  tools=23  âś…
[CASE 4: BOTH allow:['read'] AND directInvoke.hostFsRead=true]           read=true   tools=24  âś…

=== ALL PASS ===
```

CASE 2 is the critical bot-P1 case: a legacy config with `allow: ["read"]` (for whatever historical reason) stays inert at the direct-invoke layer post-merge â€” no silent host-FS read grant. CASE 4 is the only combination that grants access.

## Code-level test coverage

`src/security/audit-gateway-tools-http.test.ts` (new `describe` block):

- âś… fires (warn) when both keys set on loopback bind
- âś… escalates to critical when bind is lan
- âś… does NOT fire when only `allow: ["read"]` (legacy shape)
- âś… does NOT fire when only `hostFsRead: true`
- âś… does NOT fire on vanilla config

## Why a single fresh commit (not rebase of prior 10)

The original PR's 10 commits implemented an OLD design (single-key `allow: ["read"]` as the opt-in). The bot's P1 â€” "Require a fresh opt-in before enabling host reads" â€” needed a fundamentally different shape, not patches on top. Plus the prior commits referenced `src/agents/pi-tools.ts` which has been renamed to `src/agents/agent-tools.ts` upstream. Cleaner to reset to upstream/main and write the redesign as one commit than rebase-then-patch.

Backup of prior PR history retained at `backup/85664-pre-rebase-20260603-onto-7c1a83ff2e` on the public fork.

---

## Real-behavior proof field

**Reproducer:** `git checkout e598e2d762 && docker run --rm -v $PWD:/work -w /work --platform linux/arm64 --memory=12g node:22-bookworm-slim bash -c 'set -e; corepack enable && corepack prepare pnpm@11.2.2 --activate && node scripts/tsdown-build.mjs && node scripts/runtime-postbuild.mjs && node /work/.proof-driver-85664.mjs'` â€” exits 0 with `=== ALL PASS ===`.

**Real environment tested:** built fresh from current PR head `e598e2d762` inside a clean throwaway `node:22-bookworm-slim` arm64-native container â€” workspace bind-mounted, `node scripts/tsdown-build.mjs` produced `dist/tool-resolution-tMi-Eqaq.js`. Driver imports `resolveGatewayScopedTools` from that bundle and probes the 4 config combinations.

**Evidence after fix (terminal capture, redacted):**
```
[bundle] /work/dist/tool-resolution-tMi-Eqaq.js
[resolve] resolveGatewayScopedTools found

[CASE 1: neither gate set (vanilla)]
  config: {}
  read reachable: false
  total tools: 23

[CASE 2: only allow:['read'] set (legacy shape)]
  config: {"allowRead":true}
  read reachable: false
  total tools: 23

[CASE 3: only directInvoke.hostFsRead=true set]
  config: {"hostFsRead":true}
  read reachable: false
  total tools: 23

[CASE 4: BOTH allow:['read'] AND directInvoke.hostFsRead=true]
  config: {"allowRead":true,"hostFsRead":true}
  read reachable: true
  total tools: 24

=== SUMMARY ===
  PASS  CASE 1: neither gate set (vanilla)  (read=false)
  PASS  CASE 2: only allow:['read'] set (legacy shape)  (read=false)
  PASS  CASE 3: only directInvoke.hostFsRead=true set  (read=false)
  PASS  CASE 4: BOTH allow:['read'] AND directInvoke.hostFsRead=true  (read=true)

=== ALL PASS ===
```

**Observed result after fix (head `e598e2d762`):** All 4 cases PASS. CASE 2 â€” the bot-P1 critical case â€” confirms a legacy config with `allow: ["read"]` alone does NOT grant host-FS read on the direct-invoke surface; the new `directInvoke.hostFsRead: true` opt-in is required additionally.

**What was not tested:** Live HTTP request through `handleToolsInvokeHttp` to a running gateway. The `resolveGatewayScopedTools` driver tests the candidate-set construction (where the dual-key gating lives); the downstream policy filter + HTTP dispatch are upstream-tested code paths that operate on whatever candidate set this function returns.
target.author: simonusa

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
